sections.paragraph:
ἀφιερόω (ἱερόω ‘consecrate’) 1 aor. ἀφιέρωσα (pass. in Aeschyl., Eumen. 451; Jos., Ant. 11, 148) in H. Gk. cultic t.t. consecrate (Diod S 1, 90, 4; Plut.; Philo Bybl. [c.100 a.d.]: 790 Fgm. 1, 29 Jac. [in Eus., PE 1, 9, 29]; OGI, index, and oft. in ins; Sb 7687, 7; 4 Macc 13:13; Philo Alex.; Jos., Ant. 15, 364) ἀφιέρωσαν αὐτὸν (sc. τὸν θεόν) ἐν τῷ ναῷ they have consecrated God through the temple instead of conversely basing the consecration of the temple on God B 16:2.—DELG s.v. ἱερός.

aor. aor. = aorist

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

Aeschyl Aeschyl , V b.c.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

  1. Gk. H. Gk. = Hellenistic Greek

t.t. t.t. = terminus technicus (termini technici), technical term(s)

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

Philo Bybl. Philo Bybl. = P. of Byblos, hist., c. 100 a.d.—List 5

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

Jac. Jac. = Die Fragmente der griechischen Historiker, ed. FJacoby—Lists 5, 6

Eus. Eus. = Eusebius of Caesarea, various works; IV a.d.—List 5

OGI OGI = Orientis Graeci Inscriptiones Selectae—List 3

oft. oft. = often

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

Sb Sb = Sammelbuch griechischer Urkunden aus Aegypten—Lists 3, 4

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

  1. sc. = scilicet (one may understand, supply)

B B = Barnabas (the Letter of), II a.d., except in series of uncial witnesses, in which case B refers to Codex Vaticanus (s. also Vat.). When the abbrv. B would ambiguous, Vat. is used for the codex.—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 156
